black.description: Terminal products, cellular outcomes, equilibrium states, system endpoints

darkSkyBlue.description: Biochemical reactions, transport, modifications, transcription, translation

green.description: External signals, nutrient availability, environmental conditions, initial states

lightCyan.description: Metabolites, molecular complexes, cellular states, compounds

purple.description: Multi-component requirements, signal integration, convergence points

yellow.description: Decision points with alternative pathways (yes/no branching)

description: Yeast cell polarity in *Saccharomyces cerevisiae* is a fundamental process dictating the precise location of bud emergence and subsequent polarized growth, crucial for asymmetric cell division. This intricate process begins with **bud site selection**, where haploid cells typically exhibit axial budding (near the previous division site) and diploid cells show bipolar budding (at either pole), regulated by specific protein complexes like Bud3/4 or Rsr1/Bud1/2/5. Once a site is chosen, the small Rho-type GTPase **Cdc42** acts as a master regulator. Activated by GEFs like Cdc24, Cdc42-GTP accumulates at the future bud site, recruiting effectors such as the formin Bni1 and the Polarisome (including Spa2). This leads to the **polarization of the actin cytoskeleton**, with Bni1 nucleating actin cables and Arp2/3 nucleating actin patches, which direct secretory vesicles containing cell wall components to the growth site, facilitating bud emergence. Concurrently, the **septin ring** forms at the bud neck, acting as a scaffold for polarity factors and ensuring proper cell division later. Positive feedback loops involving Cdc42-GTP and its regulators maintain this polarized growth. Finally, as the cell prepares for cytokinesis, polarity shifts, leading to the assembly and contraction of an actin-myosin ring for cell separation, ensuring the faithful inheritance of cellular components.
mermaid:
graph TD
    A[Start Yeast Cell Polarity] --> B[Cell Cycle Progression into G1]
    B --> C[Bud Site Selection Trigger]
    C --> D{Haploid or Diploid Cell?}
    D -->|Haploid| E[Axial Budding Pathway]
    D -->|Diploid| F[Bipolar Budding Pathway]

    E --> G[Bud3 & Bud4 Localization]
    G --> H[Axial Bud Site Marker Formed]

    F --> I[Rsr1/Bud1, Bud2, Bud5 Recruitment]
    I --> J[Bipolar Bud Site Marker Formed]

    H --> K{Bud Site Established?}
    J --> K
    K -->|Yes| L[Recruitment of Cdc24 (GEF)]
    K -->|No| MA[Budding Defect, Re-evaluate]

    L --> N[Cdc24 Activates Cdc42-GDP to Cdc42-GTP]
    N --> O[Local Accumulation of Cdc42-GTP]
    O --> P[Cdc42-GTP Binds Effectors]
    P --> Q[Recruitment of Bni1 (Formin)]
    P --> R[Recruitment of Spa2/Polarisome Components]
    P --> S[Recruitment of Ste20 & Cla4 (PAK kinases)]

    Q & R & S --> T{Polarisome and Polarity Factors Assembled}

    T --> U[Bni1 Nucleates Actin Cables]
    T --> V[Cdc42-GTP Recruits Arp2/3 Complex]
    V --> W[Arp2/3 Nucleates Actin Patches]
    U --> X[Formation of Polarized Actin Cables]
    W --> Y[Formation of Polarized Actin Patches]

    X & Y --> Z{Directed Vesicle Transport & Secretion?}
    Z -->|Yes| AA[Secretory Vesicle Docking & Fusion]
    Z -->|No| AB[Loss of Polarized Growth, Defect]

    AA --> AC[Cell Wall Expansion at Bud Site]
    AC --> AD[Bud Emergence]

    AD --> AE[Septin Ring Assembly Triggered]
    AE --> AF[Cdc42-GTP & Gic2 Recruit Septins (Cdc3, Cdc10, Cdc11, Cdc12)]
    AF --> AG[Septin Filament Formation & Assembly]
    AG --> AH[Stable Septin Ring at Bud Neck]

    AH --> AI{Bud Growth Maintained & Directed?}
    AI -->|Yes| AJ[Continued Polarized Growth (Apical)]
    AI -->|No| AK[Premature Loss of Polarity, Defect]

    AJ --> AL[Cdc42-GTP Positive Feedback Loop]
    AL --> AM[Cdc42-GTP Recruits More GEFs (Cdc24)]
    AL --> AN[/Cdc42-GTP Inhibits GAPs (Rga1, Rga2, Bem3)/]

    AJ --> AO[Septin Ring Scaffolding Function]
    AO --> AP[Localizes Polarity Factors (e.g., Bni1, Gic2)]

    AJ --> AQ[Actin Cable Mediated Vesicle Transport]

    AM & AN & AP & AQ --> AR{Stable Polarity State Maintained}

    AR --> AS[Cell Cycle Progression to Mitosis/Cytokinesis]
    AS --> AT[Repolarization Signal for Cytokinesis]
    AT --> AU[Disassembly of Apical Polarity Components]
    AU --> AV[Assembly of Cytokinetic Actin Ring]
    AV --> AW[Recruitment of Myo1 (Myosin II)]
    AV --> AX[Assembly of F-actin Ring]

    AW & AX --> AY{Cytokinetic Ring Assembled}

    AY --> AZ[Actin Ring Contraction & Membrane Ingression]
    AZ --> BA[Cell Wall Resynthesis at Division Site]
    BA --> BB[Cell Division & Daughter Cell Separation]
    BB --> BC[End of Polarity Cycle]
